request.question: The title of this film is french and is a continuation of Carne, what is the film called?

title: Turner Browne
Santa Claus in film
Stephen Nicholas (actor)
The Millionaire (1931 film)
Nagesh Bhonsle
Si j'avais au moins...
I Stand Alone (film)
Devta (film)
Carne (1968 film)
Carne (film)
sentences: Turner Browne was born on July 6, 1949 in Lake Charles, Louisiana.
From 1969 to 1970 he attended Louisiana State University in Baton Rouge.
He is a self-taught photographer and has worked with all film formats as well as digital.
In 1973, the Sunflower Foundation gave him a grant which launched his career.
In 1977 "Louisiana Cajuns" was published by the Louisiana State University Press, a monograph of his documentation of the rural Cajuns; the text is in both English and French.
The same year a documentary film called "The New Klan" was released in which he was the lead cinematographer.
Two years later he was the director of photography on the feature film "Only Once in a Lifetime".
In 1987 The French Institute in New York City exhibited Louisiana Cajuns; from there the work was exhibited nationally.

In 2000 Browne donated his Louisiana Cajun collection to the Library of Congress in Washington, D.C. He co-authored "The Macmillan Dictionary of Photographic Artists and Innovators" with his wife, author Elaine Bernstein Partnow; it was published in 1983.
A monograph of his documentary work on the White River of Arkansas, "The Last River", was published in 1993; the book inspired director Jeff Nichols in his making of the film "Mud".
Currently Turner works as a photographer and videographer in Los Angeles.
Motion pictures featuring Santa Claus abound and apparently constitute their own subgenre of the Christmas film genre.
Early films of Santa revolve around similar simple plots of Santa's Christmas Eve visit to children.
In 1897, in a short film called "Santa Claus Filling Stockings", Santa Claus is simply filling stockings from his pack of toys.
Another film called "Santa Claus and the Children" was made in 1898.
A year later, a film directed by George Albert Smith in titled "Santa Claus" (or "The Visit from Santa Claus" in the United Kingdom) was created.
In this picture, Santa Claus enters the room from the fireplace and proceeds to trim the tree.
He then fills the stockings that were previously hung on the mantle by the children.
After walking backward and surveying his work, he suddenly darts at the fireplace and disappears up the chimney.
"Santa Claus' Visit" in 1900 featured a scene with two little children kneeling at the feet of their mother and saying their prayers.
The mother tucks the children snugly in bed and leaves the room.

Santa Claus suddenly appears on the roof, just outside the children's bedroom window, and proceeds to enter the chimney, taking with him his bag of presents and a little hand sled for one of the children.
He goes down the chimney and suddenly appears in the children's room through the fireplace.
He distributes the presents and mysteriously causes the appearance of a Christmas tree laden with gifts.
The scene closes with the children waking up and running to the fireplace just too late to catch him by the legs.

A 1909 film by D. W. Griffith titled "A Trap for Santa Claus" shows children setting a trap to capture Santa Claus as he descends the chimney, but instead capture their father who abandoned them and their mother but tries to burglarize the house after he discovers she inherited a fortune.

A twenty-nine-minute 1925 silent film production titled "Santa Claus", by explorer/documentarian Frank E. Kleinschmidt, filmed partly in northern Alaska, feature Santa in his workshop, visiting his Eskimo neighbors, and tending his reindeer.
A year later, another movie titled "Santa Claus" was produced with sound on De Forest Phonofilm.

Over the years, various actors have donned the red suit (aside from those discussed below), including Monty Woolley in "Life Begins at Eight-thirty" (1942), Alberto Rabagliati in "The Christmas That Almost Wasn't" (1966), Dan Aykroyd in "Trading Places" (1983), Jan Rubes in "One Magic Christmas" (1985), David Huddleston in "" (1985), Jonathan Taylor Thomas in "I'll Be Home for Christmas" (1998), and Ed Asner in "Elf" (2003).
Later films about Santa vary, but can be divided into the following themes.
Stephen Nicholas (born 23 August 1978) also known as Stephen Charles Nicholas is an actor and presenter from Doncaster, South Yorkshire, England.
Stephen currently lives in Sheffield, his first role was on Sky One's Dream Team, where he played Scott Ward.
From there, he filmed the first in the trilogy Goal!
(In which he played a Newcastle United Reserves player).
Following this, he moved to Los Angeles, where he played Smith in the feature film Futbaal: The Price of Dreams.
Stephen then returned to the UK to make a Bollywood film called Dhana Dhana Goal with John Abraham.

Stephen then experienced his first opportunity in reality TV with the show Premier League All Stars for Sky One, as well as playing a footballer, he was on-hand to present celebrity gossip and pitch side reports.
He then appeared in Celebrity Most Haunted and Date the Enemy.
From there he then went on to star in Goal 3 where he not only acted in the film he also became the football choreographer and choreographed all the football scenes in the film.

Nicholas then starred in the film Damned United where he played Welsh international Alan Durban, the film was filmed in Chesterfield and Leeds and was directed by Oscar winner Tom Hooper and also starred Oscar nominated Michael Sheen.

Stephens next production was the feature film called 'No Way Back Now'about the notorious Manchester district of Moss Side, where Stephen played the lead actor Stuart Gavin,The feature is roughly based on the notorious Gooch gang that terrorised Manchester throughout the years.
The next move for Stephen was pantomime where he was part of the production Aladdin over the Christmas period of 2015 in Doncaster playing Abanaza the main villain which he did until January 7, 2016!

He has recently been cast in the up-and-coming Feature Film 'Whiteblade' where he will play Thurstan the head Warlord Whiteblade is currently in production and Stephen is shooting his scenes in August 2016.

In September 2016 Stephen will be presenting the Sky TV show 'Britz go Bollywood' the show consists of a group of Celebrities being dressed by The best Indian designers, Stephen is the main presenter of the show which will be screened live September 2, 2016.
The Millionaire is a 1931 all-talking pre-code comedy film produced and distributed by Warner Bros. and starring George Arliss in the title role.
The film is a remake of the 1922 film called "The Ruling Passion", which also starred Arliss.
The film was based on the short story "Idle Hands" by Earl Derr Biggers.
In one of his earliest film roles, James Cagney had a brief but key appearance.
Nagesh Bhosle (also Nagesh Bhonsle or Nagesh Bhosale) is an award-winning Indian film, television and theatre actor.
He has recently worked on a Hollywood film called 'Hotel Mumbai' alongside Jason Isaacs, Armie Hammer and Dev Patel.
Nagesh has acted in more than a hundred Indian films and thousands of episodes in Television shows.
After completing endless projects in the Hindi, Marathi and Telugu language fraternity as an actor, he ventured into Directing and Producing.
He has successfully made 6 films so far, as a Director.
In 2009, his debut film 'Goshta Choti Dongraevadhi' (English title: A story as small as a mountain) was highly appreciated by the critics and audiences at the time.
Nagesh's films are enthralling and speak of the contemporary issues in India.

Ajna Motion Picture's first film "Panhala" (2015), directed and produced by Nagesh, is critically acclaimed, won many awards and featured at domestic and foreign film festivals including the 25th Golden Rooster in China.

His current film "Nati Khel" which is to release in cinemas in 2017 was invited to be screened at the PAMLA conference in Los Angeles, California and open for dialogue between the filmmakers and the scholarly patrons of PAMLA.
It has also won a special award at the Wuhan International Art Film Festival, in China, December 2016.
in 2017, it has been an official selection at Pune International Film Festival, Bodhisattva International Film Festival and Orange City International Film Festival.
"Si j'avais au moins..." (English: "If I Had at Least..." ) is a song recorded by French singer Mylène Farmer.
This pop rock ballad was written by Farmer with music composed by Laurent Boutonnat.
It was the third single released, from her seventh studio album "Point de suture" (2008), on 16 February 2009.

Directed by Bruno Aveillan, the accompanying music video is the continuation of Farmer's previous single "Dégénération" and is the last part of a short film called "The Farmer Project", broadcast in January 2009.
In the video, the singer portrays a supernatural character who saves animals from testing and heals them.

Although a survey showed that a minority of the voters wanted the song as the third single, "Si j'avais au moins..." received positive reviews and achieved some success on the French Singles Chart, debuting at number-one.
Farmer performed the song on television during the NRJ Music Awards show on 17 January 2009 and on the 2009 Mylène Farmer en tournée tour.
I Stand Alone is a 1998 French art film written and directed by Gaspar Noé, and starring Philippe Nahon, Blandine Lenoir, Frankye Pain, and Martine Audrain.
The original French title is Seul contre tous, which means "Alone against all".

The film, focusing on several pivotal days in the life of a butcher facing abandonment, isolation, rejection and unemployment, was the director's first feature-length production, and is a sequel to his 1991 short film "Carne".
Devta (Hindi: देवता ; English: Snake God ) is a 1956 Hindi partly coloured swashbuckler film written by Sadasiva Bramham and directed by Pattana.
The film had Vyjayanthimala in the title role with Gemini Ganesan and Anjali Devi in the lead while Agha, Bipin gupta, Daisy Irani, Krishna Kumari and M. N. Nambiar forms an ensemble cast.
The film was produced by Narayanan Iyengar with his production company, Narayanan Company.
The music was composed by C. Ramchandra with lyrics provided by Rajendra Krishan.
The film was a remake of hugely successful Tamil film called "Kanavaney Kankanda Deivam".
Carne is a 1968 Argentine Cult film starring Isabel Sarli.
The film is one of the most popular and controversial film of Sarli career.
The film has been called "a masterpiece of kitsch".
Carne is a 1991 French drama film written and directed by Gaspar Noé, starring Philippe Nahon and Blandine Lenoir.
It tells the story of a horse butcher with a mute daughter.
At a running time of 40 minutes, it was the first longer film directed by Noé.
The narrative was continued in Noé's 1998 full-length debut, "I Stand Alone".

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question` and `passages`, produce the single field `summary`.

Goal: compress the grounded first-hop evidence into a short intermediate state that makes it easy for the next-hop agent to (a) see what facts are present per candidate/entity, (b) see what is ambiguous or missing, and (c) know 1–4 prioritized next-hop retrieval targets or concise queries. This module does not produce the final answer unless the provided passages already unambiguously contain every fact required to resolve the question's relation for every argument.

Procedure:
1) Decide whether the question's relation is semantically COMPLETE given the supplied passages. Treat the hop as INCOMPLETE if any of the following hold: a) the relation requires combining or comparing facts across pages (e.g., comparing birth dates, publication/founding years, ordinal ranks, confirming a named director among candidates) and the passages do not supply each required fact unambiguously for all relevant arguments; b) any relation argument is ambiguous or has multiple candidate pages or mentions in the passages; c) an expected candidate page or explicit fact for an argument is missing (e.g., only one side of a comparison is present). Do not trigger INCOMPLETE solely from surface tokens — base the decision on whether the relation's arguments are unambiguously resolved.

   - Narrowed trigger for absent question-named candidates: mark a named candidate as "absent and needing a page/bridge" only when (i) that question-named candidate is a semantically required argument to resolve the relation (the relation cannot be resolved without confirming a fact about that candidate), AND (ii) the supplied passages do not already contain any unambiguous short factual token (for example, an explicit date, direct property token, or relation-token) that would fully answer this hop. If the passages already include such an explicit token that resolves the relation, do NOT mark the hop INCOMPLETE merely because a candidate page is missing.

2) If the hop is COMPLETE (all required facts to resolve the relation for every argument are present unambiguously in the passages): produce a concise derived result in `summary`. This may be a short phrase or sentence that states the necessary fact(s) needed by the pipeline. Keep it minimal and grounded in the passages; you may include a 1–2 token citation (passage title) if helpful.

3) If the hop is INCOMPLETE or AMBIGUOUS: do NOT assert a final answer or perform the final comparison/selection. Instead emit a short structured report in `summary` containing these labeled elements (prefer bullets or a compact paragraph with explicit labels). Keep all text neutral and non-assertive.

  - evidence: For each named candidate/argument mentioned in the question or passages, list the candidate label and the exact passage title(s) that mention it plus a very short quoted snippet or explicit fact token found there (1–12 words). If a candidate is not mentioned, say so briefly.

  - ambiguities_or_missing: Explicitly name any ambiguities (e.g., multiple candidates for the same name, multiple pages that could be the intended entity) and any missing expected pages or facts.

  - missing_facts_or_bridge: State exactly which fact(s) or disambiguation is needed to complete the relation (e.g., "birth date for Person A", "publication year of Work B", "director credit for Film C"). Be as specific and minimal as possible.

  - retrieval_direction: Provide 1–4 prioritized next-hop targets or concise queries to resolve the missing facts/ambiguities. Apply this dynamic prioritization policy:
      a) If the missing fact is biographical or identity information about a semantically required question-named candidate (examples: birth date, occupation, nationality, canonical identity that the relation depends on), prioritize retrieving the candidate's canonical biography or main entity page first (exact title when known).
      b) If the missing fact is a property of another entity or a document type that typically holds the property (examples: game developer, layout engine used by a site, race event date, event report), prioritize a concise property-focused query or the canonical page that directly carries that property (examples: "[Game] developer", "[Browser] engine", "2009 Belgian Grand Prix").
      c) If name-disambiguation is suspected (same or similar names present on-page or multiple candidate pages could match), explicitly note the ambiguity and prioritize an exact candidate-title retrieval and a short disambiguation query (e.g., "<Name> (occupation)" or "<Name> disambiguation").
      d) Never prefer a biography-first retrieval when the missing item is better recovered by a targeted property query or when passages already include an explicit relation token that completes the hop.

Formatting and style constraints (apply to both COMPLETE and INCOMPLETE cases):
- The output must be a single `summary` field only. Do not emit other fields.
- In INCOMPLETE reports, use neutral, non-assertive phrasing. Do not produce final comparisons, numeric answers, or definitive selections (e.g., avoid "X is the oldest" or "Answer: 3").
- Keep the report compact and actionable (short labeled bullets or one concise paragraph). Avoid long prose.
- It is allowed and encouraged to quote short factual snippets from passages and cite passage titles to show grounding. Grounding snippets must be ≤12 words.
- Do not defer or mark INCOMPLETE when the passages already unambiguously provide all facts for the relation and its arguments.

Produce the `summary` content that follows these rules.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the single field `query`.

Goal
- Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-intent query suitable for a second-hop search that will recover the missing bridging evidence needed to answer the question. Do not produce the final answer in `query`.

Semantic decision rule (when to emit a retrieval-intent query)
- If summary_1 does NOT assert a confirmed linking entity or verified bridging fact needed to answer the question, you MUST emit exactly one compact retrieval-intent query. "Does not assert a confirmed bridge" includes short or candidate-only summaries (e.g., a single named entity, a quoted candidate title, or numeric anchors that are not verified) and any case where the visible anchors do not unambiguously identify the needed bridge.
- If summary_1 already names a confirmed bridge (a clearly identified, verified linking entity or unambiguous metadata that directly connects the question's pieces), you may produce a compact query to fetch supporting evidence, but do NOT produce an answer in the `query` field.
- If summary_1 explicitly states that the bridge cannot be recovered automatically (extreme ambiguity or user-local context requiring clarification), do NOT force a misleading retrieval query. In that case, set `query` to the single short token BRIDGE_UNRECOVERABLE (all caps, no punctuation) so downstream steps can prompt the user for clarification. Do not invent facts.

Query construction rules
- Output exactly one compact line of text as `query`. Keep it short and retrieval-focused (few keywords, no full sentences). Use visible anchors from `question` and `summary_1` and minimal retrieval keywords that express the missing relation (e.g., anchor1 anchor2 which artist recorded both; AlbumTitle album artist genre; 1991–92 NHL season regular season).
- Construct the query as a single noun-phrase: a canonical anchor OR an anchor paired with a minimal disambiguator (anchor + short parenthetical/occupation/identifier) optionally followed by a single concise relation-noun when the missing bridge is a specific temporal or numeric attribute.
  - Only add a minimal disambiguator when the visible anchor is ambiguous and the disambiguator itself is explicitly present or strongly implied in `question` or `summary_1`. Do not add disambiguators when the anchor is already unambiguous or the bridge is already confirmed.
  - Relation additions are allowed only as a single neutral relation-noun that names the target attribute (examples: "birth year", "publication date", "species count"). Do NOT use verb-like or assertive relation phrasing (forbid words/phrases such as "born", "birth name", "aka", "founder", "founded", "is the").
  - The final query should look like: "Anchor" OR "Anchor Disambiguator" OR "Anchor Disambiguator relation-noun" (all as a short noun-phrase).
- Do not include the final answer, assertions, or user-facing instructions in the `query` field. Avoid polite phrasing, questions to the user, or explanatory text. Prefer noun phrases and relation keywords.
- If your natural impulse would be to write a declarative answer or a clarification sentence in `query`, instead convert that output into a compact retrieval-intent query that targets the missing bridge.
- Preserve semantic fidelity: base the query only on visible anchors and relations in `question` and `summary_1`. Do not invent hidden identifiers or assume unseen facts.

Format contract
- Return exactly one field named `query` whose value is a single short string. If unrecoverable as described above, set `query` to BRIDGE_UNRECOVERABLE.

Examples of disallowed outputs for `query`
- A declarative answer (e.g., "The artist was X.")
- A user-facing clarification sentence (e.g., "Do you mean X or Y?") unless summary_1 explicitly requires clarification — in that case use BRIDGE_UNRECOVERABLE.

Behavioral constraints
- Do not use surface heuristics (token count, punctuation, or quotation marks) as the trigger; apply the semantic rule above.
- Emit only one compact retrieval string when the bridge is missing; do not emit multiple queries or long explanations.

Inputs available: `question`, `summary_1`. Output required: single field `query`.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`. The `summary` must be a concise, post-retrieval integration of the evidence that preserves the facts needed by the final answer. Produce only the `summary` field as the integrated output string. Follow these rules when composing `summary`:

- Purpose: Integrate the first-hop `context` with the newly retrieved `passages` into a grounded state that retains or resolves the earlier bridge only when the second-hop passages supply relation- or attribute-facts that are necessary to answer or disambiguate the `question` (for example: partnership/membership links, joint-award partners indicated by 'with', or requested biographical qualifiers such as nationality, birthdate, current team/status).

- Primary output: The summary's primary output should be the concise canonical label or token drawn exactly from `context` (do not change which label/name/token was selected by earlier answer-selection logic).

- When to append a supporting sentence: Append a single short, passage-grounded supporting sentence only when all of these three conditions hold:
  1) The `question`'s semantic intent genuinely requires a relation- or attribute-fact to justify or disambiguate the canonical label from `context` (not when the question is a pure identification/token-extraction or simple binary label with no disambiguating need).
  2) At least one retrieved `passage` contains an explicit, unambiguous predication that links the exact canonical label (or a clearly corroborated alias) to the required relation or attribute. The passage must state the link directly (examples of acceptable forms: 'X is a member of Y', 'X formed in YEAR', 'X was born DATE', 'X with Y', 'X is the Y of Z'). Mere co-occurrence, implication, or mention of the attribute elsewhere on the page is insufficient.
  3) Identity alignment is verified: confirm that the passage's assertion refers to the same entity token in `context` by matching the exact name or an alias that is explicitly corroborated in the passages (via roles, dates, titles, or other unambiguous attributes). If identity is ambiguous or the passage links the attribute to a different named entity, do not append.

- Content and form of the appended sentence: If appending, produce one short sentence that (a) explicitly names the canonical label or the corroborated alias and (b) states the direct relation/attribute exactly as asserted by the supporting passage (paraphrase briefly; do not invent or speculate). Keep the added sentence tightly focused and grounded in the passage wording; avoid long quotations. The entire `summary` (primary label plus appended sentence) must be concise and limited to 1–3 sentences total.

- When not to append: Do not append a supporting sentence and instead return only the canonical label/token when any of the following apply:
  - The question expects a normalized single-token identifier or binary/multiple-choice label and no passage-stated relation is required to disambiguate it.
  - The `context` already contains a full, passage-consistent, multi-clause integrated answer for that question.
  - The supporting passage does not explicitly predicate the required relation/attribute of the same named entity/clearly corroborated alias in `context` (including cases of mere co-occurrence or indirect/mediated implication).
  - Identity alignment is ambiguous or ties the attribute to another entity.

- Transitive/alias caution: Do not collapse mediated or transitive links into a direct equivalence unless each necessary link is explicitly and unambiguously asserted in the provided passages and clearly refers to the same entity. If the relation would require chaining two separate assertions, both must be present and identity-aligned; otherwise refrain from asserting the transitive equivalence.

- Insufficient information: If `context` and `passages` together do not supply enough explicit, identity-aligned information to resolve the required relation/attribute, summarize the remaining uncertainty succinctly rather than asserting unsupported facts.

- Style and constraints: Keep output concise. Do not invent facts or add unsupported inferences. Avoid long verbatim quotations. Do not change which label or name is selected by the existing answer-selection logic. Produce only the `summary` field (a single string) as the integrated output.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Use only summary_1 and summary_2 as evidence. Treat summary_2 as the later, integrated evidence and prioritize it when reconciling information; use summary_1 only as the earlier, pre-second-hop context. Do not assume the final state is unresolved solely because summary_1 contains bridging or retrieval-oriented language.

When summary_2 lists multiple co-referent lexical variants for the same entity, prefer the shortest explicit canonical variant only if all of the following hold: (1) that shorter variant appears verbatim in summary_2; (2) removing the longer form's additional qualifiers (parentheticals, locative suffixes, administrative/categorical words, punctuation/abbreviation markers) would not delete tokens the question explicitly requires or would not create ambiguity among entities visible in the summaries; and (3) the shorter variant still uniquely identifies the intended referent within the visible summaries. If any of these conditions fails, emit the form from summary_2 exactly, preserving parentheticals, punctuation, administrative/categorical tokens, multi-component place formatting, and parenthetical acronyms. Base these choices on semantic content and uniqueness in the visible evidence; do not apply surface heuristics that blindly strip parentheses, punctuation, locative suffixes, legal or category words, or other disambiguating tokens.

Produce exactly one field, answer, whose value is the minimal final answer string that is fully supported by the visible summaries. The answer field must contain only that minimal plain-text string and nothing else (no explanation, no surrounding quotes, no JSON wrappers, no debugging or transport artifacts).

Decide whether the question's intent is binary (a truth-value / yes-no question) or non-binary (asking for a name, title, date, place, numeric count, selection, or other factual value). Make this decision semantically (based on the question's meaning), not by surface heuristics like token counts or punctuation.

- If the question's intent is binary AND both summary_1 and summary_2 unambiguously agree on the same truth-value, emit exactly one lowercase token: either yes or no (and nothing else) as the answer value.

- For all other question intents (non-binary), emit the minimal factual string supported by the summaries exactly as needed: preserve multi-token values, necessary punctuation, full date formats, and place formats (for example, full names, "City, State", or "Month Day, Year"), and do not truncate these to a single token.

If the summaries conflict, are ambiguous, or do not jointly support an unambiguous minimal answer, do not fabricate or over-assert; follow the system's established fallback behavior for ambiguous or unsupported questions.

Always forbid emitting serialized queries, JSON/dict-like structures, internal-debug wrappers, or other transport artifacts as the final answer. The answer must be plain text only.

Make all judgments about minimality and agreement based on the semantic content of summary_1 and summary_2, with priority to summary_2 when they differ.

Produce the answer value exactly as specified above in the answer field.

The film is a remake of the 1922 film called "The Ruling Passion", which also starred Arliss.
The film was based on the short story "Idle Hands" by Earl Derr Biggers.
In one of his earliest film roles, James Cagney had a brief but key appearance.
Nagesh Bhosle (also Nagesh Bhonsle or Nagesh Bhosale) is an award-winning Indian film, television and theatre actor.
He has recently worked on a Hollywood film called 'Hotel Mumbai' alongside Jason Isaacs, Armie Hammer and Dev Patel.
Nagesh has acted in more than a hundred Indian films and thousands of episodes in Television shows.
After completing endless projects in the Hindi, Marathi and Telugu language fraternity as an actor, he ventured into Directing and Producing.
He has successfully made 6 films so far, as a Director.
In 2009, his debut film 'Goshta Choti Dongraevadhi' (English title: A story as small as a mountain) was highly appreciated by the critics and audiences at the time.
Nagesh's films are enthralling and speak of the contemporary issues in India.

Ajna Motion Picture's first film "Panhala" (2015), directed and produced by Nagesh, is critically acclaimed, won many awards and featured at domestic and foreign film festivals including the 25th Golden Rooster in China.

His current film "Nati Khel" which is to release in cinemas in 2017 was invited to be screened at the PAMLA conference in Los Angeles, California and open for dialogue between the filmmakers and the scholarly patrons of PAMLA.
It has also won a special award at the Wuhan International Art Film Festival, in China, December 2016.
in 2017, it has been an official selection at Pune International Film Festival, Bodhisattva International Film Festival and Orange City International Film Festival.
"Si j'avais au moins..." (English: "If I Had at Least..." ) is a song recorded by French singer Mylène Farmer.
This pop rock ballad was written by Farmer with music composed by Laurent Boutonnat.
It was the third single released, from her seventh studio album "Point de suture" (2008), on 16 February 2009.

Directed by Bruno Aveillan, the accompanying music video is the continuation of Farmer's previous single "Dégénération" and is the last part of a short film called "The Farmer Project", broadcast in January 2009.
In the video, the singer portrays a supernatural character who saves animals from testing and heals them.

Although a survey showed that a minority of the voters wanted the song as the third single, "Si j'avais au moins..." received positive reviews and achieved some success on the French Singles Chart, debuting at number-one.
Farmer performed the song on television during the NRJ Music Awards show on 17 January 2009 and on the 2009 Mylène Farmer en tournée tour.
I Stand Alone is a 1998 French art film written and directed by Gaspar Noé, and starring Philippe Nahon, Blandine Lenoir, Frankye Pain, and Martine Audrain.
The original French title is Seul contre tous, which means "Alone against all".

The film, focusing on several pivotal days in the life of a butcher facing abandonment, isolation, rejection and unemployment, was the director's first feature-length production, and is a sequel to his 1991 short film "Carne".
Devta (Hindi: देवता ; English: Snake God ) is a 1956 Hindi partly coloured swashbuckler film written by Sadasiva Bramham and directed by Pattana.
The film had Vyjayanthimala in the title role with Gemini Ganesan and Anjali Devi in the lead while Agha, Bipin gupta, Daisy Irani, Krishna Kumari and M. N. Nambiar forms an ensemble cast.
The film was produced by Narayanan Iyengar with his production company, Narayanan Company.
The music was composed by C. Ramchandra with lyrics provided by Rajendra Krishan.
The film was a remake of hugely successful Tamil film called "Kanavaney Kankanda Deivam".
Carne is a 1968 Argentine Cult film starring Isabel Sarli.
The film is one of the most popular and controversial film of Sarli career.
The film has been called "a masterpiece of kitsch".
Carne is a 1991 French drama film written and directed by Gaspar Noé, starring Philippe Nahon and Blandine Lenoir.
It tells the story of a horse butcher with a mute daughter.
At a running time of 40 minutes, it was the first longer film directed by Noé.
The narrative was continued in Noé's 1998 full-length debut, "I Stand Alone".

It tells the story of a horse butcher with a mute daughter.  At a running time of 40 minutes, it was the first longer film directed by Noé.  The narrative was continued in Noé's 1998 full-length debut, "I Stand Alone".

Carne (1968 film) | Carne is a 1968 Argentine Cult film starring Isabel Sarli.  The film is one of the most popular and controversial film of Sarli career.  The film has been called "a masterpiece of kitsch".

I Stand Alone (film) | I Stand Alone is a 1998 French art film written and directed by Gaspar Noé, and starring Philippe Nahon, Blandine Lenoir, Frankye Pain, and Martine Audrain.  The original French title is Seul contre tous, which means "Alone against all".  The film, focusing on several pivotal days in the life of a butcher facing abandonment, isolation, rejection and unemployment, was the director's first feature-length production, and is a sequel to his 1991 short film "Carne".

Belle & Sebastian: The Adventure Continues | Belle & Sebastian: The Adventure Continues (original title: Belle et Sébastien, l'aventure continue) is a 2015 French adventure film.  It is directed by Christian Duguay.  The film is the sequel to the 2013 film "Belle and Sebastian".

Come What May (2015 film) | Come What May (original title: En mai, fais ce qu'il te plaît; also titled Darling Buds of May) is a 2015 French historical drama film directed by Christian Carion.  It stars August Diehl and Olivier Gourmet in lead roles, and was released on 4 November 2015.  The film score was composed by Ennio Morricone.

Jean-Jacques Beineix | Jean-Jacques Beineix (] ; born 8 October 1946) is a French film director and generally seen as the best example of what came to be called the "cinéma du look".  Critic Ginette Vincendeau defined the films made by Beineix and others as "youth-oriented films with high production values...The "look" of the "cinéma du look" refers to the films' high investment in non-naturalistic, self-conscious aesthetics, notably intense colours and lighting effects.  Their spectacular (studio based) and technically brilliant "mise-en-scène" is usually put to the service of romantic plots."  The "cinéma du look" included the films of Luc Besson and Léos Carax.  Luc Besson, like Beineix, was much maligned by the critical establishment during the 1980s, while Carax was much admired.  In late 2006, Beineix published a first volume of his autobiography, "Les Chantiers de la gloire" (in French only).  The title alluded to the French title of Stanley Kubrick's film, "Les Sentiers de la gloire" ("Paths of Glory").

What's in a Name? (film) | What's in a Name?  (original title: Le Prénom, literally "The Given Name") is a French-Belgian comedy film, written and directed by Alexandre de La Patellière and Matthieu Delaporte and released in 2012.  It is adapted from the play "Le Prénom" by the same authors.  The film was a box office success in France, selling 3,340,231 tickets.
response.summary_1: Seul contre tous — the original French title of I Stand Alone (the 1998 continuation of Carne). [I Stand Alone (film): "original French title is Seul contre tous"]
response.hop2_query: I Stand Alone (film) original French title
hop2_docs: I Stand Alone (film) | I Stand Alone is a 1998 French art film written and directed by Gaspar Noé, and starring Philippe Nahon, Blandine Lenoir, Frankye Pain, and Martine Audrain.  The original French title is Seul contre tous, which means "Alone against all".  The film, focusing on several pivotal days in the life of a butcher facing abandonment, isolation, rejection and unemployment, was the director's first feature-length production, and is a sequel to his 1991 short film "Carne".

Ghost in the Shell: Stand Alone Complex (PSP) | Ghost in the Shell: Stand Alone Complex, released in Japan as Kokaku Kidotai: Stand Alone Complex - Domain of the Hunters (攻殻機動隊 STAND ALONE COMPLEX -狩人の領域- , Kōkaku Kidōtai Sutando Arōn Konpurekkusu -Kariudo no Ryōiki- ) , is a first-person shooter video game based on the cyberpunk anime series "" and a sequel to the first "" game of the same title, developed by G-Artists and published for the PlayStation Portable by Sony Computer Entertainment, Atari and Bandai in 2005.  It was released in Japan on September 15, 2005, in Europe on October 21, 2005, and in North America on October 26, 2005, but under the same name as the original PS2 game causing confusion and misinterpreting as a port.

Howling IV: The Original Nightmare | Howling IV: The Original Nightmare is a 1988 direct-to-video horror film directed by John Hough from a screenplay by Freddie Rowe and Clive Turner.  Starring Romy Windsor, Michael T. Weiss, Antony Hamilton, Susanne Severeid and Lamya Derval.  "The Original Nightmare" is the fourth entry in the series of seven stand-alone films with loose continuity and not so much a sequel, but, a more faithful adaptation of Gary Brandner's source novel "The Howling" (1977) (hence the title).
Living on Love Alone | Living on Love Alone (French title: D'amour et d'eau fraîche) is a 2010 French drama film written and directed by Isabelle Czajka.

High Lane (film) | High Lane (Original French title: Vertige) is a 2009 French horror film directed by Abel Ferry.  A group of friends on vacation decide to venture onto a trail high up in the mountains that has been closed for repairs.  The climb proves more perilous than expected, especially as they soon realize that they are not alone.  The adventure turns into a nightmare.

Ghost in the Shell: Stand Alone Complex | Ghost in the Shell: Stand Alone Complex, known in Japan as Mobile Armored Riot Police: Stand Alone Complex (Kōkaku Kidōtai Sutando Arōn Conpurekkusu ) , is a Japanese anime television series produced by Production I.G and based on Masamune Shirow's manga "Ghost in the Shell".  It was written and directed by Kenji Kamiyama, with original character design by Hajime Shimomura and a soundtrack by Yoko Kanno.  The first season aired from October 2002 to October 2003 and was positively received by critics.  A second season titled Ghost in the Shell: S.A.C. 2nd GIG, known in Japan as Mobile Armored Riot Police: S.A.C. 2nd GIG (Kōkaku Kidōtai Esu Ē Shī Sekando Gigu ) , aired from January 2004 to January 2005.

Out Traveler | Out Traveler is a gay and lesbian travel magazine from the publishers of "Out" and "The Advocate", combining photography with coverage of LGBT travel topics.  Published as a stand-alone title from 2003 to 2008, it was subsequently incorporated as a supplement to "Out" magazine and is no longer available for subscriptions as a stand-alone title.
